summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of BoloDB landing page + onboarding (signup/login/connect). All 16 review-request checkpoints verified. All data-testids present, password validation & visibility toggle working, theme switch working, skip-to-content link functional, cross-navigation working, error alerts render with role='alert', mobile responsive (375x800) with no horizontal overflow, Connect page testids fully present. Backend is degraded (Postgres unavailable — expected per env note) so signup POST returns 502; the frontend surfaces this correctly in a role='alert' error message.

minor.issue: Returns 500/502 because PostgreSQL is not available (EXPECTED per environment note, degraded mode)

issues: 'SQL Server' label in the trust-strip DB list wraps onto two lines at ~375px due to gap:12px between logos — cosmetic only, still readable; consider reducing gap or shrinking font on <420px to keep the list on a single line.
Unused local variable `const before = appState.theme;` in handleThemeToggle() — dead code, minor cleanup.

action_items: (Optional cosmetic) Tighten trust-strip DB list on very narrow screens so 'SQL Server' does not wrap.
(Nit) Remove unused `const before = appState.theme` in MarketingNav.svelte handleThemeToggle().
No functional fixes needed — landing + onboarding pass all optimization/UX/data-testid/accessibility acceptance criteria in this env.
critical_code_review_comments: SignupScreen.svelte and LoginScreen.svelte contain large inline style attributes rather than scoped CSS/classes; while functional, this hurts readability and reuse. Consider extracting shared auth-form styles into a class.
ConnectScreen.svelte imports `onMount` from svelte but the file uses `$effect`/runes elsewhere — mixing runes and legacy lifecycle is fine but be consistent for maintainability.
Error UI uses hardcoded hex colors (#FFF8ED, #F5D78A, #7A5C0A) instead of theme tokens — will not adapt cleanly to a light/dark theme change on the signup/login error banners.

The `handleSupabaseGoogle` in GoogleSignIn.svelte will show 'Google sign-in is not configured' when supabase config is null — good UX; but the error surface uses plain <div> without role='alert' (login/signup have it). Consider adding role='alert' for consistency.
Positive: excellent data-testid coverage across all interactive elements, proper aria-live regions, skip-to-content link, aria-pressed on DB type buttons, autocomplete hints on all form fields.

success_rate.backend: N/A (frontend-only testing per request; backend intentionally degraded)

test_credentials: N/A - no auth credentials needed; test_credentials.md was not present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend fully verified against the 16 review-request bullets. All data-testids on landing, signup, login, connect are present. Password validation transitions correctly (green checks). Password toggle switches input type. Theme toggle switches document theme attribute (dark <-> crisp). Skip link is off-screen normally (top:-50px) and moves to top:8px on focus. Backend is degraded because PostgreSQL is not configured in this env — that is expected. No further frontend testing needed; only minor cosmetic/dead-code polish items remain.
rca of the issue: No functional issues found. Signup 502 is due to backend PostgreSQL unavailability which is by design in this preview env, and the frontend handles it gracefully via the role='alert' error banner.
